stem: A 17-year-old client is admitted for labor induction and is requesting an epidural. Based on the client's legal status, complete the bowtie by selecting the potential condition, two necessary actions, and two parameters to monitor.

tabs.title: SBAR
tabs.content: Situation: At 08:00, I am caring for a 17-year-old G1P0 at 40 weeks gestation, admitted for induction of labor. She is requesting information about epidural anesthesia and needs to sign consent forms. Background: The client is legally married and has provided a certified copy of her marriage certificate. Her parents are out of state and unavailable. She is A&O x4 and has no known allergies. Assessment: Vital signs are stable, FHR is 140 bpm with moderate variability. The client correctly states she is an emancipated minor and can provide her own consent. Recommendation: Please proceed with the informed consent process, ensuring all legal documentation is scanned into the EHR.

tabs.title: Marital Status & Legal Authorization
tabs.content:
DocumentStatusNotes
Marriage CertificateVerified (Certified Copy)Registered in current state
Photo IdentificationVerifiedBirthdate confirms Age 17
Policy ManualSection 4.2'Minors who are legally married are considered emancipated for healthcare decisions.'

tabs.title: Vital Signs
tabs.content: BP 118/72, HR 88, RR 20, T 98.6°F. Fetal Heart Rate 140 bpm.

actionOptions.text: Witness client's signature on consent forms
Verify marriage certificate in medical record

parameterOptions.text: Presence of marriage documentation in chart

hoverRationales.c1: A legally married minor is considered emancipated and has the legal authority to make their own healthcare decisions. This status removes the requirement for parental consent.
hoverRationales.c2: Medical neglect involves a parent or guardian failing to provide necessary medical care. This client is actively seeking care and has the legal right to do so, making this condition inapplicable.
hoverRationales.c3: An informed consent deficit occurs when a client lacks understanding or legal capacity. This client has the legal capacity due to emancipation; the deficit would only occur if she didn't understand the procedure.
hoverRationales.a1: As an emancipated minor, the client is the legal decision-maker. The nurse's role is to witness her signature after ensuring she has received all necessary information for informed consent.
hoverRationales.a2: The nurse must confirm the client's legal status by verifying the documentation that grants her emancipation. This is a critical step before accepting the client's own consent.
hoverRationales.a3: Seeking parental consent is inappropriate and legally unnecessary for an emancipated minor. This action would violate the client's autonomy and delay necessary care.
hoverRationales.a4: The client's husband cannot provide consent for her. Emancipation grants the client the right to consent for herself, not transfer that right to her spouse.
hoverRationales.p1: Ensuring the client understands the procedure's risks, benefits, and alternatives is a core component of informed consent. This is a crucial parameter to monitor regardless of the client's age or legal status.
hoverRationales.p2: The legal basis for the client's ability to self-consent rests on her marital status. The nurse must monitor that this documentation is present and correctly filed in the medical record.
hoverRationales.p3: The age of the client's spouse is legally irrelevant to her status as an emancipated minor or her ability to provide informed consent for her own medical care.
hoverRationales.p4: The location or availability of the biological parents is irrelevant once the client's status as an emancipated minor is legally established through marriage.
